toastAutoIntensive: Intensive mode: check about every 15 min (Vercel cron).
toastAutoDaily: Daily random UTC slot enabled — next run shown below.
toastShieldOn: Auto shield: URL patterns merged after useful rounds (middleware + legal config, not the repo).
toastShieldOff: Auto shield off: rounds no longer enrich these patterns.
vaultFailedBodyDefault: Check email or Telegram alerts. Often: missing cloud password, bad key, or the scheduled job did not run.
vaultRunningTitle: A backup may have been running
vaultRunningBody: If it stays stuck for a long time, check that the nightly cron runs and Vercel has enough time to finish.
vaultStaleTitle: It's been a while since a successful copy
vaultStaleBody: Last success: {date} UTC. Check the robot schedule (around 3 a.m. UTC) and your settings.
pageSubtitleRich: We run a <hl>security check</hl> without breaking the castle: bots look at doors; they don't steal customer secrets. The big red button stops everything before super-assist mode.
overlayNote: Score out of 100, activity log, emergency button and “super-assist” mode without dangerous magic (no one changes your code or prices on their own).
guideWhatBodyRich: A <hl>control room</hl> to see if your digital fortress is healthy: a score, a list of findings, and buttons to run checks or stop everything if you panic a little. Nothing runs without your click (except small bots you allow in settings).
guideScoreBodyRich: Like a <hl>quiz after the last inspection</hl>, not a thermometer that changes every second. If the score is low, read the list below and scan again after fixing something.
guideKillBodyRich: The <hl>big stop</hl>: it turns off automatic checks and super-assist mode, like unplugging a noisy toy. Turn it back on when you're ready.
guideGodBodyRich: The “<hl>AI gives you more ideas</hl>” mode during special missions, but <hl>it doesn't touch</hl> your code, prices, or site design on its own.
guideVaultBodyRich: That's the <hl>encrypted backup</hl> of your database, stored in a cloud vault (you need <mono>BACKUP_S3_*</mono> and <mono>SENTINEL_VAULT_AES_KEY</mono>, plus the Vault migration). If nothing shows, the robot hasn't succeeded yet.
guideRobotTitle: Robot that runs on its own
guideRobotBodyRich: You choose <hl>how often</hl> a small bot patrols. While the stop is on, it does nothing — even if the alarm rings in the background.
killBannerBody: Everything is paused on the server. When it's calm, you can turn it back on.
killHelpPastille: Like turning off the TV when it's too loud: everything that runs on its own here stops. You can turn it back on anytime.
killCardDesc: You press, and done: no more auto checks, no super-assist, until you reopen.
killTitleOn: You plug back in: bots can work again.
killTitleOff: You unplug: this whole module pauses immediately, without breaking files.
godHelpPastille: The AI talks more and suggests ideas during special missions, but it doesn't change your site, prices, or look on its own.
godCardDescRich: When it's on, you get <hl>extra tips</hl> for “ghost” missions — without the bot rewriting your files, prices, or design. The <hl>URL shield</hl> (Robot box) is separate: middleware patterns merge into legal config, not Git.
godTitleKillFirst: Remove the emergency stop first, then you can turn this back on.
godTitleGodOff: You turn off enhanced tips for next steps.
godTitleGodOn: You turn on “more ideas” mode, without dangerous auto delegation.
robotSectionTitle: Robot that runs on its own
robotIntroRich: <hl>Ghost Protocol</hl> is the guard that walks the castle on a <hl2>schedule</hl2> you pick: each pass looks at something different, without breaking walls. Then it writes what it saw in the log and updates your score. It doesn't drop files into your project on its own. This is <hl3>not a “zero hacker” promise</hl3>: limited probes (throttling, no destructive load), not a full-surface red team — the optional shield below is not a managed WAF, review, secrets, or external audit.
scheduleOffHelp: The bot does nothing on its own; you keep the buttons.
scheduleHourlyHelp: Roughly a small round each hour, if the server accepts.
scheduleDailyHelp: Once per day at a random time so load doesn't always hit the same moment.
scheduleIntensiveHelp: The bot may run often (about every 15 min) as long as the stop isn't on.
shieldToggleHelp: Conservative patterns on query only; stored in legal_config — no Git writes.
shieldDesc: When a round surfaces signals on certain pillars, safe regexes can be added to block obvious abusive loads in the URL. Targeted control — not unlimited pentest, not a replacement for managed WAF or human fixes in source.
cronNoteRich: The server alarm rings about <hl>every 15 minutes</hl>: but the little bot only runs if you allowed a schedule above.
sentinelHelpPastille: Secret copy of your whole database in a cloud vault. You need the DB migration + cloud passwords on Vercel; the robot works at night.
sentinelIntroRich: Picture a <hl>photo of your whole filing cabinet</hl>, compressed, sealed, then sent to a vault keeper (S3). Passwords are <mono>BACKUP_S3_*</mono> and <mono>SENTINEL_VAULT_AES_KEY</mono>. The nightly robot (Vercel cron + secret) does the trip without loading everything into memory at once.
sentinelNoTrace: No trace yet: run the Vault DB migration and set cloud variables on Vercel so the first copy can start.
ipNoteRich: <lead>If you move or change your box:</lead> your <hl>internet address</hl> often changes. Update the <mono>ADMIN_ALLOWED_IPS</mono> list on Vercel like a new <hl>access card</hl>, and check your contact settings — otherwise the door may refuse without warning you fast enough.
ghostBtnHelp: You manually start a “ghost” round: one step of the castle tour, then the summary goes to the log and the score.
scanBtnHelp: You request a big check right now: like opening every cupboard at once, but without breaking dishes — the score and list update.
filterEmpty: Nothing at this severity — widen the filter.
footerRich: This is mostly <hl>security and backup</hl>. To know if your site “feels fast” for real humans, use other dashboards (real user metrics), not this room alone.
